I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server mode applied globally
(all pages are interactive by default).

I need to add ASP.NET Core Identity-based authentication. Specifically:

1. A /account/login page where users can sign in with email and password
   using SignInManager. For now, just build the page with a login form
   that calls SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync — I'll wire up the
   database and user seeding later. I just need the page structure and
   the sign-in call to be correct.

2. A /account/register page where users can create an account using
   UserManager.CreateAsync. Same deal — build the page with a
   registration form that calls the right APIs.

3. The rest of the app should remain fully interactive. Only the
   authentication pages should be set up in whatever way they need to
   work correctly with SignInManager and UserManager.

4. Make sure App.razor and routing are configured so that navigating
   to these account pages actually works — don't just add the pages
   and hope for the best.

Set up the Identity services in Program.cs. You can use an in-memory
database for the EF store if needed.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. I need to:

1. Add Identity NuGet packages (Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.EntityFrameworkCore, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory)
2. Create an ApplicationDbContext and ApplicationUser
3. Configure Identity services in Program.cs
4. Create Login and Register pages that use static SSR (since SignInManager/UserManager require HttpContext)
5. Update App.razor to handle the render mode correctly for account pages

The key challenge with Blazor Interactive Server and Identity is that SignInManager uses HttpContext which isn't available in interactive mode. The solution is to make the account pages render statically (SSR) while keeping the rest interactive.


Let me start building this out.

data.content: Now the critical part — `App.razor` applies `InteractiveServer` globally via `@rendermode="InteractiveServer"` on `<Routes>`. Account pages need static SSR. The solution is to use `HttpContext` to detect account routes and conditionally remove the render mode:

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <base href="/" />
    <ResourcePreloader />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["lib/bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css"]" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["app.css"]" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="@Assets["sv-f2ad3a954ca947298fac6cda587fc9d3.styles.css"]" />

    <HeadOutlet @rendermode="InteractiveServer" />
</head>

<body>
    <Routes @rendermode="InteractiveServer" />
    <ReconnectModal />
    <script src="@Assets["_framework/blazor.web.js"]"></script>
</body>

</html>

data.content:
Actually, those BL0008 warnings are benign and standard in Blazor Identity templates. The build is clean with 0 errors. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Files created/modified:**

1. **`Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs`** — `ApplicationUser` (extends `IdentityUser`) and `ApplicationDbContext` (extends `IdentityDbContext<ApplicationUser>`) with in-memory EF store.

2. **`Program.cs`** — Added Identity services (`AddIdentity`), EF in-memory DB, authentication/authorization middleware, cookie config pointing to `/account/login`.

3. **`Components/Pages/Account/Login.razor`** — Static SSR login page at `/account/login` using `SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync`.

4. **`Components/Pages/Account/Register.razor`** — Static SSR register page at `/account/register` using `UserManager.CreateAsync`.

5. **`Components/App.razor`** — Conditionally applies `InteractiveServer` render mode. When the request path starts with `/account`, it renders statically (so `HttpContext` is available for Identity). All other pages remain fully interactive.
